summary: The session focused on discussing Dots, a no-code platform for community managers to create custom automation flows, and Spatial Canvas, a visual tool for building these flows. The user requested detailed information about Spatial Canvas, including its benefits and potential goals for community management.
facts: Dots is a no-code platform that allows community managers to create custom automation flows without coding knowledge. Spatial Canvas is a no-code solution that enables community managers to build custom automation flows using a visual representation and a drag-and-drop interface. One benefit of Spatial Canvas is that it makes it easy for non-technical users to create and manage automation flows. Spatial Canvas can improve the efficiency and effectiveness of automation flows by allowing users to visualize connections between different elements and actions. Goals for using Dots and Spatial Canvas include automating the onboarding process for new community members. Another goal is to direct members to relevant content based on their interests and needs. Streamlining the moderation process by automatically flagging potential violations is also a goal that can be achieved with these tools. Using Spatial Canvas can help identify potential problems in automation flows and improve overall performance.
